Explanation: Using `returningResult(Wallet.WALLET)` fetches all columns from the WALLET table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Consider specifying only the required columns explicitly, e.g., `.returningResult(Wallet.WALLET.UUID, Wallet.WALLET.AMOUNT)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., `dsl.select(field(CONSTRAINT_NAME), field(CHECK_CLAUSE), field(CONSTRAINT_CATALOG), field(CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA))`.
